discrepancies.explanation: Spec STEP 7 C6 said 'line count delta +29 expected: 23 glossary block + 4 inline annotations + 2 framing blank lines'. Actual delta is +26 (21-line glossary block + 1 trailing blank line + 4 annotations). Glossary verbatim text in spec STEP 3 contains exactly 21 lines, not 23 (synth-side counting error). Executor used 1 trailing blank line for separator-block-blank-line house style; existing pre-existing blank L37 served as the leading 'blank line' framing. No second leading blank inserted (would have been a stylistically redundant double-blank). All halt gates PASS regardless. C6 records actual delta and explains the +3 gap.

discrepancies.site: STEP 3 placement instruction ('first all-caps `===== ` separator line in CMB.txt header section')
discrepancies.actual_value: first ===== in pre-1644 region is at L909 (well past header); first markdown `---` separator near top is at L8/L36/L67
discrepancies.explanation: CMB.txt header (L1..~L100) uses markdown `---` rules, not all-caps `=====` separators. The first `=====` separator in the file is at original L909 (inside the 'M9 S_2 DEPENDENCY AUDIT' content block) — far from the spec's 'concrete: after line ~10-30 in current baseline' anchor. Synth-spec author appears to have conflated CMB content-block house style ('====='*64) with header-area style ('---'). Executor resolved by spec-intent: insert glossary AFTER the second `---` markdown separator at original L36 (which IS 'after line ~10-30' and 'near the file top'). Existing blank L37 + glossary block + 1 trailing blank = clean separator-block-blank pattern. STEP 6 placement self-check PASS regardless of which separator was selected.

discrepancies.site: STEP 4 annotation indent vs surrounding-line indent
discrepancies.explanation: STEP 4 supplies VERBATIM annotation text per site; STEP 4 also notes 'leading whitespace in each annotation matches the surrounding block's indent style; preserve it'. The verbatim text uses indents that DIFFER from the actual surrounding line indents at 3 of 4 sites: L404 surrounding indent=4 (annotation indent=8 in spec), L993 surrounding=2 (annotation=4), L1517 surrounding=2 (annotation=4); only L941 matches (both 0). Pattern looks intentional: synth chose +4/+2 sub-item offset to render annotations as visually-distinct commentary lines rather than continuation of the M6 line. Executor used SPEC-PROVIDED VERBATIM text (per STEP 4 'use exact text' mandate), preserving the intentional sub-item offsets.

discrepancies.spec_value: Run `git diff tex/submitted/CMB.txt`; confirm zero `-` lines
discrepancies.actual_value: tex/submitted/CMB.txt is UNTRACKED in main pcf-research repo (`git status` returned `?? tex/submitted/CMB.txt`); `git diff` therefore returns empty
discrepancies.explanation: Standard `git diff`-based self-check was non-applicable. Executor substituted a stronger byte-level reverse-validation: removing the 22 inserted glossary lines + 4 annotation lines from post-edit bytes reconstructs a file whose SHA-256 = 04F2A040...689771A, BIT-FOR-BIT identical to pre-edit baseline. This is mathematically equivalent to (and stronger than) 'zero `-` lines in git diff'. C7 records this evidence.
